title: DLRS Audit Event
description: Append-only audit event recording an action against a DLRS record. v0.4 introduces tools/emit_audit_event.py which writes events to <record>/audit/events.jsonl. See docs/COMPLIANCE_CHECKLIST.md for which legal regime each event_type maps to.

event_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this event. v0.4 emitter uses <ulid> or <uuid4>; once written it MUST NOT change.

event_type.description: One of the eight canonical lifecycle events from DLRS_ULTIMATE.md, plus 'custom' for organisation-specific extensions.

record_id.description: Target DLRS record id. Cross-record events MUST be split into per-record entries.

actor_role.description: Role of the actor that emitted the event. 'system' is reserved for automated tooling; 'subject' is the data subject themselves.

actor_id.description: Opaque actor identifier (e.g. user-uuid, reviewer-handle). Optional; required when actor_role is non-system.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at which the event was recorded. MUST be monotonically non-decreasing within a single events.jsonl file.

reason.description: Short human-readable reason. Sensitive identifiers MUST NOT be embedded here; reference evidence_ref instead.

evidence_ref.description: Optional pointer to supporting evidence (relative path inside the record or an opaque ticket id). For takedowns and consent_verified, this SHOULD be set.

prev_hash.description: SHA-256 of the previous event line (hex), to form a hash chain. The first event in a file SHOULD use null. Verifies append-only intent without external infrastructure.

hash.description: SHA-256 hex of the canonical JSON serialisation of this event with the 'hash' field omitted, prefixed with 'sha256:'.

metadata.description: Free-form structured metadata. Schema-validated content SHOULD live in named top-level fields; this is for additive context.
